truths.truth: Clarifiers separate settleable solids from treated water by gravity.
Rising sludge in a clarifier is commonly caused by denitrification.
A sludge blanket that becomes too deep can cause solids carryover.
Hydraulic overloading can wash solids out of a clarifier.
Short circuiting reduces effective settling time in a clarifier.
Mechanical scraper failure can allow sludge to accumulate.
Operators monitor sludge blanket depth to maintain clarifier performance.
